prompt: The comparison always prints "not equal" even when the user inputs "hello". What is the bug?
choices: Scanner.nextLine() returns null when the user presses Enter
== compares string references, not content; use input.equals("hello") instead
String literals must be compared with .compareTo() returning 0
The Scanner must be closed before comparing its output
explanation: == on Strings checks if both variables point to the exact same object in memory. The string read from Scanner is a different object than the literal "hello", so == returns false. Fix: use input.equals("hello") or input.equalsIgnoreCase("hello").
